headlines.title: New bodycam footage shows Henry Nowak killer's arrest and repeated lies to police
headlines.description: Henry Nowak's killer is seen lying to police in bodycam video released after a request from the BBC.

headlines.title: King becomes first monarch to reveal tax bill as royal public funding to double to £100m
headlines.description: New figures show the monarch paid £12.9m in tax last year, but do not give any detailed breakdown of how that tax was calculated.

headlines.title: King and Queen will not live in Buckingham Palace after renovations
headlines.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla will continue to reside in Clarence House, Buckingham Palace says.

headlines.title: Watch: Driver gives lift to armed officer chasing suspect
headlines.description: The chase was caught on camera as the motorist pulled over to help in Margate.

headlines.title: I'm back at home again after uni - here's how I'm making it work
headlines.description: Rising costs are forcing more young adults to live with their parents, here's how not to come to blows.

headlines.title: Starmer and home secretary in row over minister's future
headlines.description: Shabana Mahmood believes Mike Tapp should be sacked for writing an article but No 10 say he remains in post.

headlines.title: Investigation after up to 40 hospital staff access records of boy attacked by crocodile
headlines.description: Cambridge University Hospitals refers itself to the Information Commissioner over the breach.

headlines.title: Boy, 14, charged with murder after body found in search for girl in south Wales
headlines.description: The body was found during the search for a 14-year-old girl, Lilly, who was reported missing.

headlines.title: BBC DJ and presenter Trevor Nelson taking a break from work due to health issues
headlines.description: The Radio 2 and 1Xtra presenter said he was now "concentrating on getting better".

headlines.title: Quake is devastating blow at time of uncertainty
headlines.description: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

headlines.title: What we know so far
headlines.description: The two quakes have killed at least 235 people and injured more than 4,300, with more feared trapped under rubble.

headlines.title: New red heat warning in place but cooler weather on the way
headlines.description: A red warning for extreme heat remains in place for the south-east but cooler air is starting to move in from the west as Elizabeth Rizzinni explains.

headlines.title: Paris restricts alcohol consumption and sales as Europe's heatwave shifts east
headlines.description: Temperatures in Germany could hit 40C in some areas while French officials bring in alcohol restrictions to ease pressure on hospitals.

headlines.title: Inside the cold store where it's -18C in a heatwave
headlines.description: Staff at Magnavale, near Grantham, say the "giant freezer" is a great place to be in hot weather.

headlines.title: Even young and fit urged to skip runs and too many beers in heatwave
headlines.description: Cardiac arrests have gone up during very hot weather, and it's not just among the elderly and frail, experts are warning.

headlines.title: How male infertility is still not getting enough attention
headlines.description: How could the system better support men who have been told they might have a fertility issue?

headlines.title: Chris Mason: The anatomy of the prime minister's downfall
headlines.description: Sir Keir, like his predecessors Liz Truss and Boris Johnson, lost the capacity to viably govern, our political editor writes.

headlines.title: Missing Glastonbury? There's a festival on your doorstep this weekend
headlines.description: Stars like Fatboy Slim and Becky Hill will play small local venues to celebrate the UK music scene.

headlines.title: Why the trail went cold in Nancy Guthrie case
headlines.description: The lack of a breakthrough in the abduction of a famous TV anchor's mother is puzzling given the huge publicity.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: Who did Donald Trump anger after saying they begged for a photo with him?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: From 42% to 5.26% - how Scotland's World Cup hopes are fading
headlines.description: Defeat against Brazil left Scotland in a precarious position, but little has gone their way since either.

headlines.title: Why are World Cup underdogs doing so well?
headlines.description: Are the surprise results achieved by lower-ranked teams in the World Cup a matter of luck - or clever planning and execution?

headlines.title: What is SkyJo? The card game being played in the England camp
headlines.description: As England try to get their game right on the pitch, the games they play off the field are performing a key role in team bonding - as Morgan Rogers reveals to BBC Sport.

headlines.title: What each team need to reach World Cup knockouts
headlines.description: With the final round of group fixtures under way the path to the World Cup final on 19 July is starting to take shape.

headlines.title: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
headlines.description: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.

headlines.title: Serena Williams to start Wimbledon comeback against Maya Joint
headlines.description: American superstar Serena Williams will make her highly-anticipated singles comeback at Wimbledon against Australia's Maya Joint in the first round.

headlines.title: Home Office plan to use more military bases to house asylum seekers
headlines.description: Three new sites in Oxfordshire, Suffolk, and Yorkshire could house about 3,750 asylum seekers if permission is granted.

headlines.title: New CCTV footage contradicts prison death evidence
headlines.description: Footage of Allan Marshall, who died after being restrained by prison officers, has been obtained by the BBC.

headlines.title: Power banks and vapes now biggest fire risk on planes
headlines.description: Lithium battery fires are the number one safety risk to aircraft, yet the number of devices found in hold bags has nearly doubled in a year.

headlines.title: Housing board votes to freeze New York rents in victory for Mamdani
headlines.description: The decision was welcomed by many tenants - but landlords fear it will worsen housing conditions.

headlines.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
headlines.description: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: What The UK's Biggest Maternity Review Tells Us About Maternity Care
headlines.description: A review has found “systemic failures” in maternity care at a hospital in Nottingham.

headlines.title: Can Trump convince Republicans he’s won the war on Iran?
headlines.description: Republicans are critical of the US lifting sanctions against Iran

headlines.title: Inside the OnlyFans machine
headlines.description: Amber Haque reveals how OnlyFans models suffer widespread exploitation by their managers.

headlines.title: Williams given favourable Wimbledon start - who else plays who?
headlines.description: American superstar Serena Williams will make her highly-anticipated singles comeback at Wimbledon against Australia's Maya Joint in the first round.

headlines.title: Why are World Cup underdogs doing so well?
headlines.description: Are the surprise results achieved by lower-ranked teams in the World Cup a matter of luck - or clever planning and execution?

headlines.title: From 42% to 5.26% - how Scotland's World Cup hopes are fading
headlines.description: Defeat against Brazil left Scotland in a precarious position, but little has gone their way since either.

headlines.title: How to be a good tennis parent
headlines.description: Pushy parents are well documented in tennis - BBC Sport looks at when things get out of hand and what is being done to change this.
